title: No network in nested containers
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Like [others](https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/4131), me too I am looking forward to the ability of running unprivileged containers inside other unprivileged containers, which IIUC is being [worked on](https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/4698). Meanwhile I am temporarily resorting to running inside **privileged** containers, which does work (can start a container and run a command), however the inner container gets no network.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

I tried different variation of the command below, and the result is always that the inner command `apt-get update` hangs for a while, then reports `Temporary failure resolving 'archive.ubuntu.com'`. By adding the `--network=host` to the inner run, the network becomes accessible, but this is not viable for my use case.

```bash
podman run \
    -it \
    --rm \
    --volume /tmp/podman:/var/lib/containers/storage \
    --privileged \
    --cap-add CAP_SYS_ADMIN \
    --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \
    --volume /sys/fs/cgroup:/sys/fs/cgroup:ro \
    --device /dev/fuse:rw \
    docker.io/library/ubuntu:18.04 \
    bash -c "
        # Install podman
        apt-get -yqq update
        apt-get -yqq install software-properties-common uidmap
        add-apt-repository -y ppa:projectatomic/ppa
        apt-get -yqq install podman slirp4netns iptables

        # Attempt to run container-in-container
        podman run \
            -it \
            --rm \
            --privileged \
            --cap-add CAP_SYS_ADMIN \
            --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \
            --cgroup-manager cgroupfs \
            --events-backend=none \
            --device /dev/fuse:rw \
            docker.io/library/ubuntu:18.04 \
            apt-get -y update
"
```

**Describe the results you received:**

Inner container has no access to network, unless run with `--network=host`

**Describe the results you expected:**

The inner container should have access to the network.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.6.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.10.4
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.10.4
  podman version: 1.6.2
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.11.3
  CgroupVersion: v1
  Conmon:
    package: 'conmon: /usr/bin/conmon'
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.3, commit: unknown'
  Distribution:
    distribution: ubuntu
    version: "18.04"
  MemFree: 938029056
  MemTotal: 16647700480
  OCIRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: 'runc: /usr/sbin/runc'
    path: /usr/sbin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  SwapFree: 16565137408
  SwapTotal: 17036734464
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 8
  eventlogger: journald
  hostname: matteo-laptop
  kernel: 5.3.0-28-generic
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 13h 43m 35.79s (Approximately 0.54 days)
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search: null
store:
  ConfigFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 25
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions: {}
  GraphRoot: /home/matteo/podman/root
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 61
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
  VolumePath: /home/matteo/podman/root/volumes
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman/bionic,now 1.6.2-1~ubuntu18.04~ppa1 amd64 [installed]
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Physical laptop.

comments.body:
First have you tried to use buidah to build containers within locked down containers?  We have this working and images available on quay.io:buildah/stable.

https://developers.redhat.com/blog/2019/08/14/best-practices-for-running-buildah-in-a-container/

Does this satisfy you needs?

As far as running a container within a container, we usually advise to turn off most of the namespaces on the internal container.  IE --net=host.  Since you are already in a container, no reason to add more isolation,  Especially if you are running in a locked down container.

> First have you tried to use buidah to build containers within locked down containers?

Yes, but I am stuck at [some point](https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/1818). However, this issue is about a different use case (see below) since I am not trying to build containers.

> https://developers.redhat.com/blog/2019/08/14/best-practices-for-running-buildah-in-a-container/
> Does this satisfy you needs?

Studied carefully some time ago, and already implemented that way. However... see the issue I linked above.

> As far as running a container within a container, we usually advise to turn off most of the namespaces on the internal container. IE --net=host. Since you are already in a container, no reason to add more isolation, Especially if you are running in a locked down container.

In my case, the "outer" container provides a web service that allows users to run containers via a web interface. For this reason, the "inner" container network needs to be isolated, or it will conflict with the "outer" container, or with other "inner" containers.
in theory, from a nested unprivileged container it should be possible to use the full set of CNI plugins as the rootless user has full control on the network namespace in the outer container.
I am willing to provide more data and perform more tests, if it can help. Switching podman versions should be possible for me, too.
have you already tried using CNI from a rootless container (`--network bridge`)?

Just tried, no luck. But thanks to your suggestion, I also found `--network=slirp4netns`, and that one seems to work!

Not sure about the differences between the various types, and whether this one is suitable in our environment (any hint?) but I will go ahead and try to integrate this solution. Will report back as soon as possible.

slirp4netns on top of slirp4netns will be really slow.

Ideally we should use `--network host` inside of the container, or get CNI to work.

> slirp4netns on top of slirp4netns will be really slow.

I believe that this does not affect the current use case because the outer container is privileged (so it uses the bridge network?). Anyway, during my tests performance seems acceptable.

However, I am facing a different problem with `slirp4netns`: it appears that, as soon as the inner container is started, the outer container becomes unreachable (forwarded ports do not respond).

Here is a minimal reproducer (note that `podman_test` is simply a default Ubuntu image where I pre-installed podman + netcat, see the OP):
```bash
# Start the outer container in background
podman run \
    -it \
    --rm \
    --publish 127.0.0.1:8001:8000 \
    --volume /tmp/podman:/var/lib/containers/storage \
    --cap-add CAP_SYS_ADMIN \
    --cap-add CAP_NET_ADMIN \
    --volume /dev/net/tun \
    --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \
    --volume /sys/fs/cgroup:/sys/fs/cgroup:ro \
    podman_test \
    bash -c "
        # Start listening on the exposed port
        nc -lk 8000 &

        # Start the inner container (after some time)
        sleep 5
        podman run \
            -it \
            --rm \
            --cgroup-manager cgroupfs \
            --events-backend=none \
            --network=slirp4netns \
            docker.io/library/ubuntu:18.04 \
            bash -c 'sleep 10' &
" &

# Test before launching the inner container (succeeds)
sleep 2
nc -zv -w1 localhost 8001 >&2

# Test after launching the inner container (fails)
sleep 10
nc -zv -w1 localhost 8001 >&2

# Wait for background processes to exit
wait
```

> I believe that this does not affect the current use case because the outer container is privileged (so it uses the bridge network?). Anyway, during my tests performance seems acceptable.

if the outer process is rootless, it will still use slirp4netns.  `--privileged` for rootless doesn't give any additional privilege in the host for setting up a bridge
The outer container is rootful. Sorry for not being clear, I thought that privileged containers must be rootful (or they wont start).

No, I actually started to write a blog on this a while back. I should finish writing it.
--privileged means turn off all of the container security isolation. (SELinux, Dropped Capabilities, Read/Only kernel file systems, Seccomp.

It has nothing to do with Rootless or rootful, and nothing to do with namespaces.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@matpen Can I close this issue?

@rhatdan Actually, the issue does not seem to be solved. According to the [latest investigation](https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/5188#issuecomment-586707796), there seem to be no viable workaround. My understanding is that the only hope to [get CNI to work](https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/5188#issuecomment-585855545), as mentioned by @giuseppe. So, unless that task is being tracked by another issue, I would rather keep this one open.
@matpen @giuseppe No work on this in the last couple of months.
Indeed. I am unsure about the status, either: is anyone actually assigned to this issue?

the inner container will end up trying to setup the same CNI network as the outer container.

You should either change the mask for the outer container, or the inner one.

Can you try the following command?

```
podman run \
    -it \
    --rm \
    --volume /tmp/podman:/var/lib/containers/storage \
    --privileged \
    --cap-add CAP_SYS_ADMIN \
    --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \
    --volume /sys/fs/cgroup:/sys/fs/cgroup:ro \
    --device /dev/fuse:rw \
        localhost/podman_test \
    bash -c "
        sed -i -e 's/10.88.0/10.89.0/g' /etc/cni/net.d/87-podman-bridge.conflist
        # Attempt to run container-in-container
        podman run \
            -it \
            --rm \
            --privileged \
            --cap-add CAP_SYS_ADMIN \
            --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \
            --cgroup-manager cgroupfs \
            --events-backend=none \
            --device /dev/fuse:rw \
            docker.io/library/ubuntu:18.04 \
            apt-get -y update
"
```

Please notice the additional sed command

the `sed` command is the proposed fix for the problem, I'd not expect it to work without it :-)

and I don't think podman should try to deal this case.  It is a misconfiguration from the user side.
